rulings.Case Title: DENNIS RUSH VS. WHITE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 11, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DENNIS RUSH, DAVID FLAHERTY, CHRISTOPHER FLAMING, FRANK MCGUYER, MICHAEL DEFELICE Motion To Compel And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss consequences of lack of verified responses and issues of relevance to the subject matter. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PETROS BERKE VS. MIGUEL CHACON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Carla Parra
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 11, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF PETROS BERKE Motion To Compel Deposition Of Carla Parra. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. Court lacks jurisdiction over nonparties with respect to a motion based on service of motion by mail. (See Lund v. Superior Court (1964) 61 Cal.2d 698, 713 [requiring contempt procedures]; In re Marriage of Lemen (1981) 114 Cal.App.3d 769, 780 [allowing motion where non-party appeared at deposition].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice, because of service by mail was less than requisite 16 court days plus additional 5 days for mailing. rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN MOORE VS. RICHARD BURDEN TEED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion 1) For Additional Time To Complete The Deposition Of Defendant Richard Burden Teed And 2) To Compel Attendance
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 11, 2014, Line 14, PLAINTIFF JUSTIN MOORE's Motion 1) For Additional Time To Complete The Deposition Of Defendant Richard Burden Teed And 2) To Compel Attendance. rulings.Case Title: ADELAIDA GONGORA VS. MOHAMMAD, NADEEM, D/B/A CITY PIZZA AND YUCATECAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order That Matters In Req For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 11, 2014, Line 6,PLAINTIFF ADELAIDA GONGORA's Moion For Order That Matters In Requuest For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted. Plaintiff's Motion for Matters in Requests for Admissions be Deemed Admitted is Granted. The Court considered Defendants' Opposition, which was filed two days late, and the Supplemental Declaration of Matthew J. Webb, which was filed on July 29, 2014. Defendants' opposition fails to show that verified responses were in fact served on the plaintiff. The exhibits only provided the first page of the responses and the proof of service. The Supplemental Declaration of Matthew J. Webb shows that the responses served on Plaintiff counsel on June 30, 2014 were unverified. "An unverified response is ineffective; it is the equivalent of no responses at all." Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2014) 8:1364.1 citing Appleton v. Superior Court (1988) 206 CA3d 632, 636. The Declaration of Matthew J. Webb is not persuasive. The trial date is August 18, 2014. No motion has been filed to continue the trial. The discovery was served on May 2, 2014. According to defense counsel's declaration, he did not learn that defendant Nadeem Mohammad was out of the country until three weeks after the verified responses were due. Furthermore, counsel for defendants did not seek an order to extend the time to provide verified responses. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendants Nadeem Mohammad and Shabbir Mughal is granted pursuant to CCP §2033.280(c). Sanctions in the amount of $700.00 are to be made payable to Adelaida Gongora and her attorneys of record, Liberation Law Group no later than August 18, 2014. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ADELAIDA GONGORA VS. MOHAMMAD, NADEEM, D/B/A CITY PIZZA AND YUCATECAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses In Supp Of Monetary Sanction Req
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 11, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ADELAIDA GONGORA's Motion To Compel Responses In Support Of Monetary Sanction Request. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to: (1) Form Interrogatories, Set #1-General; (2) Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set #1, Special Interrogatories Set, #1; and Request for Production of Documents, Set #1 on Nadeem Mohammad dba City Pizza and Yucatecan Food and Shabbir Mughal are granted. The Court considered Defendants' Opposition, which was filed two days late, and the Supplemental Declaration of Matthew J. Webb, which was filed on July 29, 2014. Defendants' opposition fails to show that verified responses were in fact served on the plaintiff as the exhibits only provided the first page of the responses and the proof of service. The Supplemental Declaration of Matthew J. Webb shows that the responses served on Plaintiff counsel on June 30, 2014 were unverified. "An unverified response is ineffective; it is the equivalent of no responses at all" Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2014) 8:1113 citing Appleton v. Superior Court (1988) 206 CA3d 632, 636. The Declaration of Matthew J. Webb is not persuasive. The trial date is August 18, 2014. Defendants and their counsel were aware of the trial date since they were served with the trial date notice by the court on January 8, 2014. No motion has been filed to continue the trial.. Furthermore, the discovery was served on May 2, 2014. According to defense counsel's declaration, he did not learn that defendant Nadeem Mohammad was out of the country until three weeks after the verified responses were due. Furthermore, counsel for defendants did not seek an order to extend the time to provide verified responses. Defendants Nadeem Mohammad dba City Pizza and Yucatecan Food and Shabbir Mughal to serve verified responses without objection and produce documents responsive to the discovery no later than August 15, 2014. Service shall be made by hand delivery or overnight service. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendants Nadeem Mohammad and Shabbir Mughal is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $2,000 are to be made payable to Adelaida Gongora and her attorneys of record, Liberation Law Group no later than August 18, 2014. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: 3738 GEARY BLVD. OWNERS' ASSOCATION VS. PING SAI LING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 11, 2014, Line 7, CROSS DEFENDANTS PING LING and TEH LIU's AMENDED DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. Cross-Defendants only set forth arguments in their Memorandum of Points and Authorities as to why the prayer for punitive damages should be stricken. Cross-Defendants set forth no arguments about why the fifth cause of action fails to state a cause of action or why it is uncertain. The Cross-Complaint adequately pleads facts sufficient to state a cause of action for fraud. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
